posts.text:
The annual chance of being murdered in a foreign-born terrorist attack during the second Trump administration is about one in 85 million per year, according to Cato’s @AlexNowrasteh.  

ow.ly/7aZA50YuTho

The Cato Institute welcomed Federal Reserve Board Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman for a conversation on her comprehensive review of the bank capital framework and Basel III.

She shared that “These changes to the capital framework eliminate overlapping requirements, right-size calibrations to match actual risk, and comprehensively address long-standing gaps in our prudential framework. The result is more efficient regulation and banks that are better positioned to support economic

Kevin Warsh’s nomination hearing for Fed chair is the moment for lawmakers to press on key issues. Cato's @JaiKedia offers questions that would make the hearing truly useful: 
ow.ly/bTLG50Yvub6

Consumer Technology Association @CTATech CEO @GaryShapiro emphasized the importance of Section 230 law for smaller tech companies at the Cato Institute event. 

Watch the discussion on our YouTube: piped.video/live/ZB1-S8UWtMo

Video

Medicare Advantage reallocates subsidies from the sick to the healthy, says Cato’s @mfcannon. He explains that turning Medicare into a cash-transfer program would help contain spending, improve health care quality, and expand choice.

ow.ly/9Egs50Yvqw6

Both parties are competing to exempt larger amounts of income from federal taxation. But the US tax code already does so—and does so unevenly, says Cato’s @adamnmichel. 

A wiser reform would simplify the system by consolidating deductions and credits into a new uniform deduction that treats similar taxpayers more consistently.  

ow.ly/kf4r50YvobS

When those spending the money don’t bear the cost, waste and abuse follow. Welfare fraud scandals in the US prove that. 

Federal taxpayers fund the programs, but states run them—with little incentive to curb waste. 

Reform requires accountability, says Cato’s @RominaBoccia.

Video

“Just 28% of the people detained by ICE right now were convicted of any crime. This is no surprise. DHS has abandoned its public safety mission,” said Cato’s @David_J_Bier in testimony on sanctuary cities before the Senate Budget Committee. 

Read full remarks: 
ow.ly/wrJW50YtzRz

.@David_J_Bier comments on his exchange with Sen. John Kennedy, R. La., during the Senate Budget Committee hearing last week: “It’s unfortunate that senators find learning about this administration’s ethnic cleansing fantasies more revolting than the existence of those fantasies.” 
ow.ly/BinA50Yv1ag

Mamdani’s politics are grounded in an ideology that first arose from the frustrations of the early industrial era. But going backwards is not the answer, says Cato's @chellivia. Life in pre-industrial New York City was worse than a modern person can imagine.
cato.org/commentary/think-pr…

Mohamed Bailor Jalloh’s dastardly attack is one of five deadly foreign-born terrorist attacks during the second Trump administration that have killed a total of eight people.  

Foreign-born terrorism is still a small threat in Trump’s second term, Cato’s @AlexNowrasteh explains. 

ow.ly/5v0Y50YuT2L

Senator Ron Wyden emphasized the Cato Institute’s role in tech policy debates: “One of the first calls we make when an important issue comes up in the tech space is to Cato,” he said at Cato’s Section 230 at 30 event. Watch the full conversation here: piped.video/live/ZB1-S8UWtMo

Video

This punishment of Anthropic for its protected speech violated the First Amendment, says Cato’s @Thomas_A_Berry.  

Cato has joined the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF), the Chamber of Progress, and the First Amendment Lawyers Association (FALA) to file an amicus brief in federal court supporting Anthropic. 

ow.ly/hNXp50YtJtN

The United States owes almost $700 million more in interest for each month that US Customs delays the IEEPA tariff refunds, Cato’s @scottlincicome reports. The interest clock is ticking. 

ow.ly/T3U750YtOVm

“Immigrants—even illegal immigrants—are half as likely to be incarcerated for crimes. According to DHS, only about 3% of illegal immigrants have any criminal convictions,” said Cato’s @David_J_Bier in testimony before the Senate Budget Committee. 

Read full remarks: 
ow.ly/tGhb50YtzNi

Trump’s push to scrap the WTO’s “most favored nation” rule risks sparking global trade wars, raising prices, and hurting American jobs. MFN drives growth—abandoning it would make the US poorer, not freer, says Cato’s James Bacchus. 
ow.ly/nV0o50YtOYK

While the US trade deficit with China has narrowed since 2016, the US trade deficit with other major trading partners has widened from 2016 to 2024. 

Learn more in a new research analysis by Cato’s James Bacchus ➡️
ow.ly/mtcR50Yt1rF

Do the rich pay taxes? In the US, they do. And a lot of them.  

America has the most progressive tax system in the developed world, where high earners pay the largest share.  

If spending keeps rising, taxes will too.  The only solution is lower spending, says Cato’s @adamnmichel.

Video

The Trump administration should never have imposed tariffs under IEEPA. As Cato scholars now explain, the least the US government can do now is to quickly issue blanket tariff refunds.

ow.ly/Urfw50YtOU8

Published 250 years ago this month, “The Wealth of Nations” by Adam Smith remains among the most influential economics books ever written—laying the intellectual foundation for free markets, capitalism, and the classical liberal and libertarian traditions.
